1	# Segmentation — OA7-4L (task-2026-07-13-005)
2	
3	Full-pipeline segmentation driver (`microct_analysis.stages.segmentation`,
4	threshold → marker-based watershed → seed assignment → prune → sanity) plus a
5	residual-body naming pass. Deliverables: a labeled knee scene (`labels.nii.gz`,
6	labels 1–9) and per-structure masks/geometry. **Femur and tibia — the two bones
7	that carry both ratio indices — are measured and independently verified; every
8	other structure is auto-proposed or escalated for review.**
9	
10	## Threshold (subphase: pass, medium)
11	- Intake established the histogram is **NOT bimodal** on this HU-rescaled scan
12	  (RescaleSlope 0.3911 / Intercept −1000; air ≈ −941, soft-tissue ≈ −35, bone
13	  shoulder ~5000–6000). The Scanco *unitless* thresholds (220/270/320) are on the
14	  wrong scale here. The **Amira mineralized-bone** parameters are scale-appropriate.
15	- The histogram method would raise `histogram-not-bimodal`, so **manual thresholds**
16	  were supplied: **mask = 2500 HU** (Otsu = 2563, near-exact agreement), **marker =
17	  3500 HU**. `filtered.nii.gz` = median-filtered (iters=3, radius=1), isotropic
18	  10.5 µm (no resample needed). Bone mask ≈ 16.9 M voxels, traces the cortical shell.
19	- Recorded in `metadata.json.threshold_observations`.
20	
21	## Watershed (subphase: pass, high)
22	- **First pass → `needs-seeds` (`ambiguous-bone-identity`), exactly as intake
23	  predicted.** At marker = 3000 the femur and tibia dense cores fuse into one
24	  10.5 M-voxel marker component spanning the whole z-axis — no watershed split is
25	  possible. At **marker = 3500** the cores separate into distinct z-clustered
26	  components (femur ~5.0 M @ high-z, tibia ~3.8 M @ low-z).
27	- **Critical curation:** the driver's `superior/inferior` naming is `z<half / z>=half`,
28	  which is **opposite** to this scan's anatomy (femur is HIGH z). Auto-proposed
29	  seeds therefore *swapped* the labels. Seeds were curated to **femur = component
30	  z574 (high-z, bicondylar)**, **tibia = component z229 (low-z, single plateau)**,
31	  and the seeded re-run produced `status: ready`.
32	- A persistent bridging marker component (~2.9 M vox spanning the joint) is **not
33	  seeded**; watershed divides it at the joint constriction (~z380).
34	- Post-clean (largest-CC per bone): **femur CC = 1, tibia CC = 1**; interface
35	  localized (z-overlap 12.6 % of the smaller bone; femur touches only `z_max`,
36	  tibia only `z_min` = FOV crop faces). Anatomy matches the reference
37	  (`bone-split__femur-tibia-fibula`): femur superior/bicondylar, tibia
38	  inferior/plateau, meeting in a thin joint band. Evidence: `split_verification.png`.
39	
40	## Structure assignment (subphase: pass, medium)
41	- **Bicondylar discriminator (mandatory): PASS** — fem_frac 0.85 vs tib_frac 0.50
42	  (min 0.60, margin 0.15). The femur-labeled bone is the clearly-more-bicondylar
43	  one; identity is confirmed by geometry, not by z-position or volume order.
44	- **Sesamoid/osteophyte protection of femoral width: clean** — femur condyle ML
45	  edges x∈[109, 375]; **0** sesamoid/osteophyte voxels lie lateral to those edges,
46	  and those bodies are separate labels, so they cannot inflate the femur-label width.
47	- Named from **position-invariant** anatomy: **fibula** (label 9) = the long
48	  lateral low-x bone that defines the lateral side; **ossa_sesamoidea** (label 8) =
49	  large free-standing dense periarticular body (fabella-class, ~0.72 mm³) + small
50	  dense bodies — the confounder, labeled as its own structure so it is never read
51	  as osteophyte or swept into condyle width. **medial/lateral osteophytes** (6/7) =
52	  small dense residual bodies at the joint margin, split by ML side (**proposed**).
53	- Scene render `labeled_scene.png` matches the reference layout.
54	
55	### Escalated / not segmented (needs review — do NOT treat as measured)
56	- **Patella (label 3): not segmented.** No reliable free-standing anterior body was
57	  separable from the femur (it may be merged into the trochlea), and the
58	  **anterior–posterior axis is not yet locked** — the skill forbids AP-dependent
59	  naming against a guessed axis. Escalated to the orientation/landmark phase.
60	- **Menisci (4/5): not segmented.** A lower-threshold (≥1500 HU) joint-gap pass
61	  produced a spurious ~300 k-voxel partial-volume blob; emitting it as a measured
62	  volume would be worse than omitting it ("do not invent its boundary"). Escalated.
63	- **Osteophyte vs peri-meniscal identity** at the joint margin also depends on the
64	  locked AP axis — the 6/7 labels are proposals only.
65	
66	## Impact on downstream measurements
67	- **Femoral W/L and tibial IIOC H/W** (the two OA ratio indices) rest entirely on
68	  the femur and tibia labels, which are clean (CC=1) and verified. Ready.
69	- **Patella volume** and **peri-meniscal volumes** require the escalated structures
70	  — the orientation phase must lock the AP axis and confirm/segment them before
71	  those volumes can be reported.
72	
73	## Known limits
74	- Fibula (context, ~4.8 mm³) is large and may include lateral cortical bone the
75	  watershed left unassigned; it is not a measured structure, but review should
76	  confirm it did not steal the tibial lateral wall (tibial IIOC width).
77	- All non-measured structures are auto-proposed; the reference pack is one specimen
78	  + published figures, so flag any anatomy it doesn't cover.
79	
80	## Key artifacts
81	- `artifacts/labels.nii.gz` (+ `artifacts/segmentation/`), `artifacts/masks/`,
82	  `structure_assignments.json` (with `review_flags`), `geometry.json` (1.58 MB,
83	  6 decimated meshes), `filtered.nii.gz`, `seeds.json` (curated), `metadata.json`,
84	  `stage_report.json` (status ready, confidence medium).
85	- Evidence: `probe_mips.png`, `probe_zprofile.png`, `residuals.png`,
86	  `split_verification.png`, `labeled_scene.png`.
87

# Segmentation — OA7-4L (task-2026-07-13-005)

Full-pipeline segmentation driver (`microct_analysis.stages.segmentation`,
threshold → marker-based watershed → seed assignment → prune → sanity) plus a
residual-body naming pass. Deliverables: a labeled knee scene (`labels.nii.gz`,
labels 1–9) and per-structure masks/geometry. **Femur and tibia — the two bones
that carry both ratio indices — are measured and independently verified; every
other structure is auto-proposed or escalated for review.**

## Threshold (subphase: pass, medium)
- Intake established the histogram is **NOT bimodal** on this HU-rescaled scan
  (RescaleSlope 0.3911 / Intercept −1000; air ≈ −941, soft-tissue ≈ −35, bone
  shoulder ~5000–6000). The Scanco *unitless* thresholds (220/270/320) are on the
  wrong scale here. The **Amira mineralized-bone** parameters are scale-appropriate.
- The histogram method would raise `histogram-not-bimodal`, so **manual thresholds**
  were supplied: **mask = 2500 HU** (Otsu = 2563, near-exact agreement), **marker =
  3500 HU**. `filtered.nii.gz` = median-filtered (iters=3, radius=1), isotropic
  10.5 µm (no resample needed). Bone mask ≈ 16.9 M voxels, traces the cortical shell.
- Recorded in `metadata.json.threshold_observations`.

## Watershed (subphase: pass, high)
- **First pass → `needs-seeds` (`ambiguous-bone-identity`), exactly as intake
  predicted.** At marker = 3000 the femur and tibia dense cores fuse into one
  10.5 M-voxel marker component spanning the whole z-axis — no watershed split is
  possible. At **marker = 3500** the cores separate into distinct z-clustered
  components (femur ~5.0 M @ high-z, tibia ~3.8 M @ low-z).
- **Critical curation:** the driver's `superior/inferior` naming is `z<half / z>=half`,
  which is **opposite** to this scan's anatomy (femur is HIGH z). Auto-proposed
  seeds therefore *swapped* the labels. Seeds were curated to **femur = component
  z574 (high-z, bicondylar)**, **tibia = component z229 (low-z, single plateau)**,
  and the seeded re-run produced `status: ready`.
- A persistent bridging marker component (~2.9 M vox spanning the joint) is **not
  seeded**; watershed divides it at the joint constriction (~z380).
- Post-clean (largest-CC per bone): **femur CC = 1, tibia CC = 1**; interface
  localized (z-overlap 12.6 % of the smaller bone; femur touches only `z_max`,
  tibia only `z_min` = FOV crop faces). Anatomy matches the reference
  (`bone-split__femur-tibia-fibula`): femur superior/bicondylar, tibia
  inferior/plateau, meeting in a thin joint band. Evidence: `split_verification.png`.

## Structure assignment (subphase: pass, medium)
- **Bicondylar discriminator (mandatory): PASS** — fem_frac 0.85 vs tib_frac 0.50
  (min 0.60, margin 0.15). The femur-labeled bone is the clearly-more-bicondylar
  one; identity is confirmed by geometry, not by z-position or volume order.
- **Sesamoid/osteophyte protection of femoral width: clean** — femur condyle ML
  edges x∈[109, 375]; **0** sesamoid/osteophyte voxels lie lateral to those edges,
  and those bodies are separate labels, so they cannot inflate the femur-label width.
- Named from **position-invariant** anatomy: **fibula** (label 9) = the long
  lateral low-x bone that defines the lateral side; **ossa_sesamoidea** (label 8) =
  large free-standing dense periarticular body (fabella-class, ~0.72 mm³) + small
  dense bodies — the confounder, labeled as its own structure so it is never read
  as osteophyte or swept into condyle width. **medial/lateral osteophytes** (6/7) =
  small dense residual bodies at the joint margin, split by ML side (**proposed**).
- Scene render `labeled_scene.png` matches the reference layout.

### Escalated / not segmented (needs review — do NOT treat as measured)
- **Patella (label 3): not segmented.** No reliable free-standing anterior body was
  separable from the femur (it may be merged into the trochlea), and the
  **anterior–posterior axis is not yet locked** — the skill forbids AP-dependent
  naming against a guessed axis. Escalated to the orientation/landmark phase.
- **Menisci (4/5): not segmented.** A lower-threshold (≥1500 HU) joint-gap pass
  produced a spurious ~300 k-voxel partial-volume blob; emitting it as a measured
  volume would be worse than omitting it ("do not invent its boundary"). Escalated.
- **Osteophyte vs peri-meniscal identity** at the joint margin also depends on the
  locked AP axis — the 6/7 labels are proposals only.

## Impact on downstream measurements
- **Femoral W/L and tibial IIOC H/W** (the two OA ratio indices) rest entirely on
  the femur and tibia labels, which are clean (CC=1) and verified. Ready.
- **Patella volume** and **peri-meniscal volumes** require the escalated structures
  — the orientation phase must lock the AP axis and confirm/segment them before
  those volumes can be reported.

## Known limits
- Fibula (context, ~4.8 mm³) is large and may include lateral cortical bone the
  watershed left unassigned; it is not a measured structure, but review should
  confirm it did not steal the tibial lateral wall (tibial IIOC width).
- All non-measured structures are auto-proposed; the reference pack is one specimen
  + published figures, so flag any anatomy it doesn't cover.

## Key artifacts
- `artifacts/labels.nii.gz` (+ `artifacts/segmentation/`), `artifacts/masks/`,
  `structure_assignments.json` (with `review_flags`), `geometry.json` (1.58 MB,
  6 decimated meshes), `filtered.nii.gz`, `seeds.json` (curated), `metadata.json`,
  `stage_report.json` (status ready, confidence medium).
- Evidence: `probe_mips.png`, `probe_zprofile.png`, `residuals.png`,
  `split_verification.png`, `labeled_scene.png`.

}

  color: var(--ink);
  line-height: 1.45;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  overflow: hidden;
}
.topbar { padding: 0.7rem 1rem 0.55rem; background: var(--card); border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border); }
.topbar h1 { margin: 0; font-size: 1rem; letter-spacing: -0.01em; }
.topbar-sub { margin: 0.2rem 0 0; font-size: 0.72rem; }
.topbar-sub a { color: var(--muted); font-family: var(--mono); text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 1px dotted var(--border); }
.topbar-sub a:hover { color: var(--accent); border-bottom-color: var(--accent); }

/* --- Evidence banner: big result, cutoff, delta, status ------------------ */
.evidence { display: flex; gap: 0.9rem; align-items: stretch; flex-wrap: wrap; padding: 0.7rem 1rem; background: var(--card); border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border); }
.ev-metric {
  display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.15rem;
  padding: 0.5rem 0.9rem; border-radius: 12px; flex: 1 1 260px; min-width: 0;
  background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--state) 9%, var(--card));
  border: 1px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--state) 36%, var(--border));
}
.ev-label { font-size: 0.74rem; color: var(--muted); }
.ev-value { font-size: 2rem; font-weight: 700; line-height: 1.05; color: var(--state); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; letter-spacing: -0.02em; }
.ev-value .ev-unit { font-size: 0.8rem; font-weight: 600; color: var(--muted); }
.ev-cut { font-size: 0.76rem; color: var(--ink); font-family: var(--mono); }
.ev-cut b { color: var(--state); }
.ev-status {
  display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.6rem; flex: 1 1 200px;
  padding: 0.5rem 0.9rem; border-radius: 12px;

# Segmentation — OA7-4L (task-2026-07-13-005)

Full-pipeline segmentation driver (`microct_analysis.stages.segmentation`,
threshold → marker-based watershed → seed assignment → prune → sanity) plus a
residual-body naming pass. Deliverables: a labeled knee scene (`labels.nii.gz`,
labels 1–9) and per-structure masks/geometry. **Femur and tibia — the two bones
that carry both ratio indices — are measured and independently verified; every
other structure is auto-proposed or escalated for review.**

## Threshold (subphase: pass, medium)
- Intake established the histogram is **NOT bimodal** on this HU-rescaled scan
  (RescaleSlope 0.3911 / Intercept −1000; air ≈ −941, soft-tissue ≈ −35, bone
  shoulder ~5000–6000). The Scanco *unitless* thresholds (220/270/320) are on the
  wrong scale here. The **Amira mineralized-bone** parameters are scale-appropriate.
- The histogram method would raise `histogram-not-bimodal`, so **manual thresholds**
  were supplied: **mask = 2500 HU** (Otsu = 2563, near-exact agreement), **marker =
  3500 HU**. `filtered.nii.gz` = median-filtered (iters=3, radius=1), isotropic
  10.5 µm (no resample needed). Bone mask ≈ 16.9 M voxels, traces the cortical shell.
- Recorded in `metadata.json.threshold_observations`.

## Watershed (subphase: pass, high)
- **First pass → `needs-seeds` (`ambiguous-bone-identity`), exactly as intake
  predicted.** At marker = 3000 the femur and tibia dense cores fuse into one
  10.5 M-voxel marker component spanning the whole z-axis — no watershed split is
  possible. At **marker = 3500** the cores separate into distinct z-clustered
  components (femur ~5.0 M @ high-z, tibia ~3.8 M @ low-z).
- **Critical curation:** the driver's `superior/inferior` naming is `z<half / z>=half`,
  which is **opposite** to this scan's anatomy (femur is HIGH z). Auto-proposed
  seeds therefore *swapped* the labels. Seeds were curated to **femur = component
  z574 (high-z, bicondylar)**, **tibia = component z229 (low-z, single plateau)**,
  and the seeded re-run produced `status: ready`.
- A persistent bridging marker component (~2.9 M vox spanning the joint) is **not
  seeded**; watershed divides it at the joint constriction (~z380).
- Post-clean (largest-CC per bone): **femur CC = 1, tibia CC = 1**; interface
  localized (z-overlap 12.6 % of the smaller bone; femur touches only `z_max`,
  tibia only `z_min` = FOV crop faces). Anatomy matches the reference
  (`bone-split__femur-tibia-fibula`): femur superior/bicondylar, tibia
  inferior/plateau, meeting in a thin joint band. Evidence: `split_verification.png`.

## Structure assignment (subphase: pass, medium)
- **Bicondylar discriminator (mandatory): PASS** — fem_frac 0.85 vs tib_frac 0.50
  (min 0.60, margin 0.15). The femur-labeled bone is the clearly-more-bicondylar
  one; identity is confirmed by geometry, not by z-position or volume order.
- **Sesamoid/osteophyte protection of femoral width: clean** — femur condyle ML
  edges x∈[109, 375]; **0** sesamoid/osteophyte voxels lie lateral to those edges,
  and those bodies are separate labels, so they cannot inflate the femur-label width.
- Named from **position-invariant** anatomy: **fibula** (label 9) = the long
  lateral low-x bone that defines the lateral side; **ossa_sesamoidea** (label 8) =
  large free-standing dense periarticular body (fabella-class, ~0.72 mm³) + small
  dense bodies — the confounder, labeled as its own structure so it is never read
  as osteophyte or swept into condyle width. **medial/lateral osteophytes** (6/7) =
  small dense residual bodies at the joint margin, split by ML side (**proposed**).
- Scene render `labeled_scene.png` matches the reference layout.

### Escalated / not segmented (needs review — do NOT treat as measured)
- **Patella (label 3): not segmented.** No reliable free-standing anterior body was
  separable from the femur (it may be merged into the trochlea), and the
  **anterior–posterior axis is not yet locked** — the skill forbids AP-dependent
  naming against a guessed axis. Escalated to the orientation/landmark phase.
- **Menisci (4/5): not segmented.** A lower-threshold (≥1500 HU) joint-gap pass
  produced a spurious ~300 k-voxel partial-volume blob; emitting it as a measured
  volume would be worse than omitting it ("do not invent its boundary"). Escalated.
- **Osteophyte vs peri-meniscal identity** at the joint margin also depends on the
  locked AP axis — the 6/7 labels are proposals only.

## Impact on downstream measurements
- **Femoral W/L and tibial IIOC H/W** (the two OA ratio indices) rest entirely on
  the femur and tibia labels, which are clean (CC=1) and verified. Ready.
- **Patella volume** and **peri-meniscal volumes** require the escalated structures
  — the orientation phase must lock the AP axis and confirm/segment them before
  those volumes can be reported.

## Known limits
- Fibula (context, ~4.8 mm³) is large and may include lateral cortical bone the
  watershed left unassigned; it is not a measured structure, but review should
  confirm it did not steal the tibial lateral wall (tibial IIOC width).
- All non-measured structures are auto-proposed; the reference pack is one specimen
  + published figures, so flag any anatomy it doesn't cover.

## Key artifacts
- `artifacts/labels.nii.gz` (+ `artifacts/segmentation/`), `artifacts/masks/`,
  `structure_assignments.json` (with `review_flags`), `geometry.json` (1.58 MB,
  6 decimated meshes), `filtered.nii.gz`, `seeds.json` (curated), `metadata.json`,
  `stage_report.json` (status ready, confidence medium).
- Evidence: `probe_mips.png`, `probe_zprofile.png`, `residuals.png`,
  `split_verification.png`, `labeled_scene.png`.

<!doctype html>
<!--
  spatial-3d review-artifact TEMPLATE (design §3.D). Self-contained, offline,
  G1–G9-valid with the PLACEHOLDER data below. The author starts from this file
  and replaces the three data blocks (window.REVIEW_MANIFEST, REVIEW_EVIDENCE,
  REVIEW_GEOMETRY) with values derived from THIS run's verified disk outputs —
  keeping the security shell, manifest schema, and the real three.js scene. The
  primary view is the 3D scene (OrbitControls); slices are optional drill-down.
-->
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1, viewport-fit=cover" />
<meta name="color-scheme" content="light" />
<title>LabRat — spatial 3D review</title>
<style>
:root {
  --ink: #12161b;
  --paper: #f3f5f8;
  --card: #ffffff;
  --border: #d3d9e0;
  --muted: #48525d;
  --accent: #1f57c3;
  --mono: ui-monospace, "SF Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  --sans: -apple-system, "Segoe UI", system-ui, sans-serif;
}
* , *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; }
html, body { height: 100%; }
body {
  margin: 0;
  font-family: var(--sans);
  background: var(--paper);
  color: var(--ink);
  line-height: 1.45;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  overflow: hidden;
}
.topbar { padding: 0.7rem 1rem 0.55rem; background: var(--card); border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border); }
.topbar h1 { margin: 0; font-size: 1rem; letter-spacing: -0.01em; }
.topbar-sub { margin: 0.2rem 0 0; font-size: 0.72rem; }
.topbar-sub a { color: var(--muted); font-family: var(--mono); text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 1px dotted var(--border); }
.topbar-sub a:hover { color: var(--accent); border-bottom-color: var(--accent); }

/* --- Evidence banner: big result, cutoff, delta, status ------------------ */
.evidence { display: flex; gap: 0.9rem; align-items: stretch; flex-wrap: wrap; padding: 0.7rem 1rem; background: var(--card); border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border); }
.ev-metric {
  display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.15rem;
  padding: 0.5rem 0.9rem; border-radius: 12px; flex: 1 1 260px; min-width: 0;
  background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--state) 9%, var(--card));
  border: 1px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--state) 36%, var(--border));
}
.ev-label { font-size: 0.74rem; color: var(--muted); }
.ev-value { font-size: 2rem; font-weight: 700; line-height: 1.05; color: var(--state); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; letter-spacing: -0.02em; }
.ev-value .ev-unit { font-size: 0.8rem; font-weight: 600; color: var(--muted); }
.ev-cut { font-size: 0.76rem; color: var(--ink); font-family: var(--mono); }
.ev-cut b { color: var(--state); }
.ev-status {
  display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.6rem; flex: 1 1 200px;
  padding: 0.5rem 0.9rem; border-radius: 12px;
  background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--state) 13%, var(--card));
  border: 1px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--state) 45%, var(--border));
}
.ev-status-icon { display: grid; place-items: center; width: 36px; height: 36px; border-radius: 9px; background: var(--state); color: #fff; font-size: 1.05rem; flex: none; }
.ev-status-text { display: flex; flex-direction: column; }
.ev-status-main { font-weight: 700; font-size: 0.92rem; color: var(--state); }
.ev-status-sub { font-size: 0.76rem; color: var(--muted); }

.tabs { display: flex; gap: 0.35rem; padding: 0.5rem 1rem 0; background: var(--paper); }
.tabs button {
  font: inherit; font-size: 0.82rem; font-weight: 600;
  min-height: 44px; padding: 0 0.9rem;
  border: 1px solid var(--border); border-bottom: none;
  border-radius: 10px 10px 0 0; background: #e7ebf1; color: var(--muted); cursor: pointer;
}
.tabs button.active { background: var(--card); color: var(--accent); border-color: var(--border); }

.stage { flex: 1; min-height: 0; padding: 0 1rem 0.5rem; display: flex; flex-direction: column; }
.panel { flex: 1; min-height: 0; background: var(--card); border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 0 10px 10px 10px; }
.panel[hidden] { display: none !important; }

#panel-3d { position: relative; display: flex; }
#scene3d { flex: 1; min-height: 320px; position: relative; overflow: hidden; }
#scene3d-canvas { width: 100%; height: 100%; display: block; touch-action: none; border-radius: 0 10px 10px 10px; background: radial-gradient(120% 120% at 50% 30%, #20242c 0%, #0c0e12 100%); cursor: grab; }
#scene3d-canvas.grabbing { cursor: grabbing; }

/* --- DOM label + leader-line overlay (replaces sprite labels) ------------ */
#leader-layer { position: absolute; inset: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%; pointer-events: none; z-index: 2; }
#label-layer { position: absolute; inset: 0; pointer-events: none; z-index: 3; }
.lm-label {
  position: absolute; transform: translate(-50%, -50%); pointer-events: auto;
  font-size: 0.72rem; font-weight: 600; color: #fff; background: rgba(14,18,24,0.9);
  border: 1px solid var(--lm, #fff); border-radius: 7px; padding: 0.18rem 0.42rem;
  white-space: nowrap; box-shadow: 0 1px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.45); max-width: 46%;
  overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis;
}
.lm-label.dim { opacity: 0.62; font-weight: 500; }
.mm-badge {
  position: absolute; transform: translate(-50%, -50%); pointer-events: none;
  font-family: var(--mono); font-size: 0.68rem; font-weight: 700; color: #12161b;
  background: #ffd24d; border: 1px solid #caa02a; border-radius: 6px; padding: 0.1rem 0.36rem; white-space: nowrap;
}
.sel-ring {
  position: absolute; transform: translate(-50%, -50%); pointer-events: none;
  width: 30px; height: 30px; border-radius: 50%; border: 2.5px solid #fff;
  box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--lm, #fff), 0 0 12px var(--lm, #fff);
  animation: sel-pulse 1.6s ease-in-out infinite;
}

` under the topbar. 2. Replaced the placeholder `window.REVIEW_GEOMETRY` literal with the sentinel `"__REVIEW_INJECT:REVIEW_GEOMETRY__"` (large data → server-side injection). 4. Appended a small per-phase script (after the template's core script, clearly marked as non-template) that renders `REVIEW_EVIDENCE.structural` into `#structure-notes` as plain text — `textContent` only, no `innerHTML`/`eval`/network/export. ## Evidence sources, per displayed claim | Displayed claim | Source (path relative to `artifacts/`) | Field | |---|---|---| | Sample id `task-2026-07-13-005` | `task.json` (task id) | — | | 3D meshes (femur, tibia, medial/lateral osteophytes, ossa_sesamoidea, fibula) | `segmentation/geometry.json` @ `6870f38374172617739d7908f8e07cf78e44f81c812c4a88f4ba408165da8960` | `meshes..vertices`/`.faces` (injected verbatim via `data_sources`, `transform: identity`) | | Orientation legend (X lateral / Y AP-unresolved / Z proximal-distal) | `segmentation/structure_assignments.json` | `.polarity` | | "Connected components — femur 1, tibia 1" | `segmentation/structure_assignments.json` @ 0.15)" | same file | `.verification.bicondylar_discriminator` | | "Sesamoid/osteophyte voxels lateral to femur condyle edges: 0" | same file |
